sessions.prompt:
You are drafting content for Arc-Factor Capital (a fictional Toronto quantitative investment firm). Read the brief at ./inputs/arcfactor-brief-1.md and draft the requested memo.

If a skill is available at ./skill/, first follow its Brain Protocol: read ./skill/SKILL.md, then ./skill/references/_index.md, patterns.md, decisions.md, log.md. If patterns.md contains any ArcFactor brand-style rules from prior sessions, apply them.

Write the memo to ./out-brand-1.md in the CURRENT directory (not inside ./skill/ or ./inputs/). That's it for this session - no checker to run, no patterns to log. This is the baseline first-pass draft.

assertions.text: out-brand-1.md exists
run brand-voice checker and capture its JSON sidecar
session 1 allowed ceiling: at most 8 of 10 rules violated (baseline - no guidance)
assertions.check: exec:test -f out-brand-1-check.json && awk -F'"count":' 'NF>1 { split($2,a,/[^0-9]/); if (a[1]+0 <= 8) exit 0; exit 1 }' out-brand-1-check.json

assertions.text: The memo reads as a plausible, professional investor memo (regardless of brand compliance). Score 1-10 for prose quality; pass if >= 5.

sessions.prompt:
Draft Arc-Factor's customer newsletter using ./inputs/arcfactor-brief-2.md.

If a skill is available, read its Brain Protocol files first (patterns.md, decisions.md, log.md).

User feedback on session 1's draft (READ CAREFULLY):
"Three corrections on the memo you sent: (1) the firm is 'ArcFactor' - one word, capital A and F. Not 'Arc Factor' or 'Arc-Factor'. (2) the product is 'PulseIndex' - CamelCase compound, no space. Not 'Pulse Index'. (3) dates must be ISO format like 2026-04-20, never 'April 20, 2026' or similar. Please don't make these mistakes again."

CRITICAL for skill arms: this feedback reveals 3 of Arc-Factor's hidden style rules. Log each rule as an imperative lesson to patterns.md before drafting, because session 3+ will NOT restate this feedback - your patterns.md is the only channel that carries rules forward.

Write to ./out-brand-2.md. No checker to run, no post-write steps.
sessions.retention_check: Smart arm's patterns.md should contain at least 3 entries by end of session 2 (logged from prompt feedback). Session 3 will not restate these rules - they will only live in the brain.

assertions.text: out-brand-2.md exists
run brand-voice checker and capture its JSON sidecar
session 2 allowed ceiling: at most 6 of 10 rules violated (rules 1, 2, 5 were disclosed)
assertions.check: exec:test -f out-brand-2-check.json && awk -F'"count":' 'NF>1 { split($2,a,/[^0-9]/); if (a[1]+0 <= 6) exit 0; exit 1 }' out-brand-2-check.json

assertions.text: rule-1 (brand spelling) must not appear - disclosed in this session's prompt feedback
rule-2 (product) must not appear - disclosed in this session's prompt feedback
rule-5 (dates) must not appear - disclosed in this session's prompt feedback
The newsletter reads as a plausible customer-facing newsletter. Score 1-10 for prose quality; pass if >= 5.

sessions.prompt:
Draft Arc-Factor's weekly market commentary using ./inputs/arcfactor-brief-3.md.

If a skill is available, read its Brain Protocol files first.

User feedback on session 2's draft (READ CAREFULLY):
"Two more corrections: (a) our CIO is 'Dr. Maira Ostrowski' - the first reference must include 'Dr.' and her first name is spelled 'Maira' (not 'Mira' or 'Maria'). (b) we say 'customer', never 'client' or 'user'. Please apply these consistently going forward."

CRITICAL for skill arms: BEFORE drafting, read ./skill/references/patterns.md. Prior session's rules (brand, product, dates) are NOT restated in this prompt - they only live in your brain. Log the two new rules from this prompt to patterns.md as well.

Write to ./out-brand-3.md.
sessions.retention_check: Smart arm's patterns.md should contain at least 5 entries by end of session 3. Smart arm should hit zero rule-1/2/5 violations (brain) AND zero rule-3/6 violations (current prompt feedback). Flat arm can only hit rules 3/6; will re-break rules 1/2/5.

assertions.text: out-brand-3.md exists
run brand-voice checker and capture its JSON sidecar
session 3 allowed ceiling: at most 4 of 10 rules violated
assertions.check: exec:test -f out-brand-3-check.json && awk -F'"count":' 'NF>1 { split($2,a,/[^0-9]/); if (a[1]+0 <= 4) exit 0; exit 1 }' out-brand-3-check.json

assertions.text: BRAIN-RETENTION TEST: rule-1 (brand) must not appear - session 1's prompt disclosed this but THIS prompt does not; only patterns.md carries it
BRAIN-RETENTION TEST: rule-2 (product) must not appear - brain-only signal
BRAIN-RETENTION TEST: rule-5 (dates) must not appear - brain-only signal
rule-3 (founder) must not appear - disclosed in THIS prompt
rule-6 (customer/client) must not appear - disclosed in THIS prompt
The commentary reads as a plausible weekly market update. Score 1-10; pass if >= 5.

sessions.prompt:
Draft Arc-Factor's signal-decay blog post using ./inputs/arcfactor-brief-4.md.

If a skill is available, read its Brain Protocol files first.

User feedback on session 3's draft (READ CAREFULLY):
"Five more rules to apply, and this is the final batch: (a) CAD amounts are formatted 'CAD 1,000,000' with a 'CAD' prefix, comma thousands - never use '$', 'USD', or 'C$'. (b) percentages must be spelled out as 'percent' - never use the '%' symbol. (c) every document MUST END on a line that reads exactly '— ArcFactor Capital' - nothing after it, em-dash plus space plus the phrase (Unicode em-dash U+2014). (d) NEVER name specific competing firms (Bridgewater, AQR, Renaissance, Two Sigma, Citadel, etc.) - always refer to competitors as 'a major incumbent' or similar generic phrase. (e) NEVER use the abbreviation 'AI' as a standalone word - always expand to 'machine learning' or 'quantitative models'. Please apply all five and don't repeat prior session errors."

CRITICAL for skill arms: your brain should already contain the rules from sessions 1-2 feedback (brand, product, dates, founder, customer). Log these five new rules to patterns.md too. This is the final session with any feedback - sessions 5 and 6 will test retention.

Write to ./out-brand-4.md.
sessions.retention_check: Smart arm's patterns.md should now contain at least 10 entries (all 10 rules logged). Flat arm can apply only the 5 rules from THIS prompt; it will re-break sessions 1-2 rules (1/2/3/5/6).

assertions.text: out-brand-4.md exists
run brand-voice checker and capture its JSON sidecar
session 4 allowed ceiling: at most 2 of 10 rules violated
assertions.check: exec:test -f out-brand-4-check.json && awk -F'"count":' 'NF>1 { split($2,a,/[^0-9]/); if (a[1]+0 <= 2) exit 0; exit 1 }' out-brand-4-check.json

assertions.text: BRAIN-RETENTION TEST: rule-1 (brand) must not appear - brain-only signal after S1
BRAIN-RETENTION TEST: rule-3 (founder) must not appear - brain-only signal after S2
BRAIN-RETENTION TEST: rule-6 (customer/client) must not appear - brain-only signal after S2
The post reads as a plausible technical-but-accessible blog post on signal decay. Score 1-10; pass if >= 5.

If a skill is available at ./skill/, first follow its Brain Protocol: read ./skill/SKILL.md, then ./skill/references/_index.md, patterns.md, decisions.md, log.md. If patterns.md contains any Liana-voice rules from prior sessions, apply them.

Write the blurb to ./out-launch-1.md in the CURRENT directory (not inside ./skill/ or ./inputs/). That's it for this session — no checker to run, no patterns to log. This is the baseline first-pass draft before any feedback.

assertions.text: out-launch-1.md exists
run launch-voice checker and capture its JSON sidecar
session 1 allowed ceiling: at most 11 of 13 rule units violated (baseline — no guidance yet)

assertions.text: The blurb reads as a plausible ~120-word indie-launch blurb (regardless of voice compliance). Score 1-10 for prose quality; pass if >= 5.

sessions.prompt:
Draft Liana's Queuedeck launch blurb using ./inputs/indie-brief-4.md.

If a skill is available, read its Brain Protocol files first.

Liana's feedback on session 3's draft (READ CAREFULLY):
"Final batch of rules, five more notes: (a) don't use 'intuitive' — empty word. (b) don't use 'effortless' — empty word. (c) don't use 'revolutionary' or any 'revolutionize' variant — never true. (d) don't use 'game-changer' or 'game-changing'. (e) don't use 'cutting-edge'. And two positive moves I want in every blurb from now on: include a first-person sentence (e.g. 'I built this because...') and name one honest limitation (e.g. 'no mobile yet', 'doesn't sync across devices'). Please apply all seven and don't repeat prior session errors."

NOTE TO SKILL ARMS: your brain should already contain every rule from earlier feedback rounds. Read ./skill/references/patterns.md — earlier rules are not restated here; patterns.md is the only channel that carries them. Log these 7 new rules to patterns.md too. This is the final session with any feedback — sessions 5 and 6 will test retention.

Write to ./out-launch-4.md.
